expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: Output is a research plan with phases, not a tiered search matrix. No Tier 1/2/3 structure present

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories (e.g., npm, GitHub, Google Scholar, StackOverflow)
expectations.evidence: Plan lists generic search terms without specifying target engines or engine-specific operators

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as stars:>100, language:typescript, or site: filters
expectations.evidence: No engine-specific operators used. Queries are plain text phrases like 'offline first sync framework'

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: No tier structure exists. Phases are organized by research objective, not by search scope escalation

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: No per-query acceptance criteria. Phase-level expected outputs are general (e.g., 'a list of 5-8 frameworks')

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: No Runtime Recovery section. A Risks section exists but describes general project risks, not search fallbacks

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
Queries specifically reference CRDT, operational transform, or offline-sync terminology
expectations.evidence: Multiple search terms reference CRDT, operational transform, and offline-first terminology

expectations.text: At least one query targets latency or performance benchmark data
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 mentions benchmarks but queries are generic ('CRDT sync latency benchmark') without engine targeting

expectations.text: Matrix covers at least 3 of the named frameworks (Automerge, Yjs, PowerSync, ElectricSQL)
expectations.evidence: Plan mentions Automerge and Yjs in search terms but does not structure queries to cover all named frameworks systematically
